operationName: Operation Swords of Iron (Israel) / October 7 War
heroHeadline: Israel Strikes Police Checkpoints Across Gaza, Killing 12; UN Human Rights Chief: Gaza Unsafe Six Months After Ceasefire
heroSubtitle: UN High Commissioner Türk condemns daily strikes as ceasefire violations; 12 killed Apr 24–25 in Khan Younis, Gaza City, Beit Lahiya; UN Women: 38,000+ women & girls killed Oct 2023–Dec 2025; 72,580+ killed since Oct 7, 2023; 796+ killed since Oct 2025 ceasefire; aid still at 37% of needed levels
footerNote: Data sourced from IDF, Hamas, UN OCHA, UNRWA, WHO, ICJ, ICC, Times of Israel, and Haaretz. Gaza death toll: 72,580+ confirmed killed since Oct 7, 2023 (Gaza Ministry of Health / WAFA, Apr 25, 2026). 796+ killed since Oct 10, 2025 ceasefire framework (Gaza MoH Apr 25, 2026). 2,400+ ceasefire violations documented by Gaza GMO. ICC arrest warrants issued for Netanyahu and Gallant (Nov 2024). Board of Peace established Jan 2026 under UN Resolution 2803. UN High Commissioner Türk: Palestinians across Gaza unsafe six months after ceasefire announcement (Apr 25, 2026). UN Women (Apr 25, 2026): 38,000+ women and girls killed Oct 2023–Dec 2025 (avg. 47/day). EU-UN Joint Recovery and Reconstruction Assessment (Apr 21, 2026): $71.4B needed over 10 years; $35.2B infrastructure damage; 371,888 housing units damaged/destroyed; human development set back 77 years. OCHA Apr 23 report: aid pallets improving (17,400/week Apr 14–20) but still 37% of required levels. 2026 Flash Appeal $4.1B req, only 9.7% funded. 12 killed Apr 24–25 in coordinated IDF strikes on police checkpoints in Khan Younis (7 dead), Gaza City Sheikh Radwan (3 dead), and Beit Lahiya tank shelling (2 dead).
